findings.evidence: Line 120 fixes the surjectivity proof to `(fun h => ⟨h, rfl⟩)`, while `mem_comap` at line 75 shows membership is independent of which `hf` is used.
findings.fix: State the lemma with an arbitrary `hf` (or first add a proof-irrelevance lemma for the `hf` argument) and derive the displayed special case from it if needed.
findings.issue: `comap_id` only rewrites `I.comap (BialgHom.id R H) (fun h => ⟨h, rfl⟩)`, so the identity law is unavailable for an arbitrary proof `hf : Function.Surjective (BialgHom.id R H)`.

findings.evidence: Lines 128-130 state `(I.comap g hg).comap f hf = I.comap (g.comp f) (hg.comp hf)`, but the proof at lines 131-132 uses only `mem_comap`, so it does not depend on the particular composite proof.
findings.fix: Generalize the RHS to `I.comap (g.comp f) hgf` for an arbitrary `hgf : Function.Surjective (g.comp f)`, or add and use proof-irrelevance for `comap`'s surjectivity argument.
findings.issue: `comap_comap` only targets the composite proof `(hg.comp hf)`, making the composition law too special when downstream code already has another witness of `Function.Surjective (g.comp f)`.

summary: The comap construction is at the intended surjective level, but two simp/API lemmas are under-generalized by hard-coding particular surjectivity proofs. Since the proof argument is not mathematical data, these lemmas should work for arbitrary witnesses.
